diagnostic_explanation: Both reviewers kept the same unsupported label and used no citations, so there is no citation difference. The substantive difference is how they interpret the available evidence relative to the claim. Option 1 treats the evidence bundle as bearing against the claim: the presence of a new regression and an ongoing investigation need makes the assumption that the old fix is still sufficient untenable, so the reason code central_proposition_unsupported fits the stated rationale better. Option 2 instead frames the problem as absence of direct evidence about continuing sufficiency, using insufficient_evidence. That is less adequate here because the supplied evidence is not merely silent; it points to a current problem in the same area and thus undercuts the assumption. Confidence does not differ because both are high.
